title: Landes (department)

infobox.key: • [President of the Departmental Council](/wiki/List_of_presidents_of_departmental_councils_(France) "List of presidents of departmental councils (France)")

infobox.value: French Land Register data, which exclude estuaries and lakes, ponds and glaciers larger than 1 km2 | |

(French pronunciation: [[lɑ̃d]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-WikiLucas00-Landes.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-WikiLucas00-Landes.wav"); [Gascon](/wiki/Gascon_language "Gascon language") and [Occitan](/wiki/Occitan_language "Occitan language"): *Lanas* [[ˈlanəs]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Occitan "Help:IPA/Occitan"); [Basque](/wiki/Basque_language "Basque language"): *Landak*) is a [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"), Southwestern [France](/wiki/France "France"), with a long coastline on the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") to the west. It also borders [Gers](/wiki/Gers "Gers") to the east, [Pyrénées-Atlantiques](/wiki/Pyr%C3%A9n%C3%A9es-Atlantiques "Pyrénées-Atlantiques") to the south, [Lot-et-Garonne](/wiki/Lot-et-Garonne "Lot-et-Garonne") to the north-east, and [Gironde](/wiki/Gironde "Gironde") to the north. Located on the [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") coast, it had a population of 433,570 as of 2023.[[3]](#cite_note-pophist-3) Its [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_in_France "Prefectures in France") is [Mont-de-Marsan](/wiki/Mont-de-Marsan "Mont-de-Marsan").

outgoing_links.target_title: Lac de Biscarrosse et de Parentis

**Landes** (French pronunciation: [[lɑ̃d]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-WikiLucas00-Landes.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-WikiLucas00-Landes.wav"); [Gascon](/wiki/Gascon_language "Gascon language") and [Occitan](/wiki/Occitan_language "Occitan language"): *Lanas* [[ˈlanəs]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Occitan "Help:IPA/Occitan"); [Basque](/wiki/Basque_language "Basque language"): *Landak*) is a [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"), Southwestern [France](/wiki/France "France"), with a long coastline on the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") to the west. It also borders [Gers](/wiki/Gers "Gers") to the east, [Pyrénées-Atlantiques](/wiki/Pyr%C3%A9n%C3%A9es-Atlantiques "Pyrénées-Atlantiques") to the south, [Lot-et-Garonne](/wiki/Lot-et-Garonne "Lot-et-Garonne") to the north-east, and [Gironde](/wiki/Gironde "Gironde") to the north. Located on the [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") coast, it had a population of 433,570 as of 2023.[[3]](#cite_note-pophist-3) Its [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_in_France "Prefectures in France") is [Mont-de-Marsan](/wiki/Mont-de-Marsan "Mont-de-Marsan").

The department is the second-largest department in France and it covers the [Forest of Landes](/wiki/Landes_forest "Landes forest"). The southwestern part of the department is part of the wider conurbation of [Biarritz](/wiki/Biarritz "Biarritz") and [Bayonne](/wiki/Bayonne "Bayonne") across the Pyrénées-Atlantique border.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Landes is one of the original 83 departments that were created during the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution") on 4 March 1790. It was created from parts of the [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_France "Provinces of France") of [Guyenne](/wiki/Guyenne "Guyenne") and [Gascony](/wiki/Gascony "Gascony").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

During the first part of the 19th century, large parts of the department were covered with poorly drained [heathland](/wiki/Heath_(habitat) "Heath (habitat)") ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *lande*); this is the origin of the department's name. The vegetation covered rich soil and was periodically burned off, leaving excellent pasturage for [sheep](/wiki/Sheep "Sheep"), which around 1850 are thought to have numbered between 900,000 and 1,000,000 in this area. The sheep were managed by [shepherds](/wiki/Shepherds "Shepherds") who moved around on stilts and became proficient at covering long distances in boggy conditions after rain.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Most of the sheep departed during the second half of the nineteenth century when systematic development of large [pine](/wiki/Pine_tree "Pine tree") plantations transformed the landscape and the local economy.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

One of the most famous individuals closely related to Landes was the 19th-century French economist [Frédéric Bastiat](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9d%C3%A9ric_Bastiat "Frédéric Bastiat").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The Nobel Prize–winning novelist [François Mauriac](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Mauriac "François Mauriac") set his novels in the Landes.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The Landes is part of the current region of [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine"). With an area stretching over more than 9000 km2, Landes is, after Gironde, the second largest department of the [metropolitan French territory](/wiki/Metropolitan_France "Metropolitan France").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

It is well known for the [Côte d'Argent](/wiki/C%C3%B4te_d%27Argent "Côte d'Argent") beach. Côte d'Argent is Europe's longest beach, and attracts many [surfers](/wiki/Surfer "Surfer") to [Mimizan](/wiki/Mimizan "Mimizan") and [Soorts-Hossegor](/wiki/Soorts-Hossegor "Soorts-Hossegor") each year. It is also home to a château called [Château de Gaujacq](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Gaujacq "Château de Gaujacq") that was built in 1686.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

* [![A view of Soorts-Hossegor](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Woman_on_sandy_beach_%28Unsplash%29.jpg/120px-Woman_on_sandy_beach_%28Unsplash%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Woman_on_sandy_beach_(Unsplash).jpg "A view of Soorts-Hossegor")

  A view of [Soorts-Hossegor](/wiki/Soorts-Hossegor "Soorts-Hossegor")
* [![The Landes forest in Contis](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Contis_vue_du_phare_4.jpg/120px-Contis_vue_du_phare_4.jpg)](/wiki/File:Contis_vue_du_phare_4.jpg "The Landes forest in Contis")

  The [Landes forest](/wiki/Landes_forest "Landes forest") in [Contis](/wiki/Contis "Contis")
* [![The Lake of Biscarrosse](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/BordLac.jpg/120px-BordLac.jpg)](/wiki/File:BordLac.jpg "The Lake of Biscarrosse")

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Politics")]

### Departmental Council of Landes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Departmental Council of Landes")]

The president of the Departmental Council has been Xavier Fortinon of the [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") since 2017. He succeeded former president of the National Assembly [Henri Emmanuelli](/wiki/Henri_Emmanuelli "Henri Emmanuelli") upon his death.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

| Party | | Seats |
| --- | --- | --- |
| • | [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") | 17 |
| • | [Left Front](/wiki/Left_Front_(France) "Left Front (France)") | 3 |
|  | [Union of the Republican Right and Independents](/wiki/Miscellaneous_Right "Miscellaneous Right") | 10 |

### National representation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: National representation")]

In the [2024 legislative election](/wiki/2024_French_legislative_election "2024 French legislative election"), Landes elected the following members of the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(France) "National Assembly (France)"):

| Constituency | | Member[[8]](#cite_note-8) | Party |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | [Landes's 1st constituency](/wiki/Landes%27s_1st_constituency "Landes's 1st constituency") | [Fabien Lainé](/wiki/Fabien_Lain%C3%A9 "Fabien Lainé") | [MoDem](/wiki/Democratic_Movement_(France) "Democratic Movement (France)") |
|  | [Landes's 2nd constituency](/wiki/Landes%27s_2nd_constituency "Landes's 2nd constituency") | [Lionel Causse](/wiki/Lionel_Causse "Lionel Causse") | [Renaissance](/wiki/Renaissance_(French_political_party) "Renaissance (French political party)") |
|  | [Landes's 3rd constituency](/wiki/Landes%27s_3rd_constituency "Landes's 3rd constituency") | [Boris Vallaud](/wiki/Boris_Vallaud "Boris Vallaud") | [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") |

In the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_(France) "Senate (France)"), Landes is represented by [two members](/wiki/List_of_senators_of_Landes "List of senators of Landes"): Éric Kerrouche and [Monique Lubin](/wiki/Monique_Lubin "Monique Lubin"). Both have served since the [2017 Senate election](/wiki/2017_French_Senate_election "2017 French Senate election").

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Economy")]

### Agriculture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Landes_(department)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Agriculture")]

Landes is known for its large [pine](/wiki/Pinus_pinaster "Pinus pinaster") forest which is the raw material for a [timber](/wiki/Timber "Timber") and [resin](/wiki/Resin "Resin") industries in the region. The forest was planted in the early nineteenth century to prevent erosion of the region's sandy soil by the sea.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
